---
title: "Getting Started"
---

Welcome! If you are a new user, this page will get you up to speed with what LISSY is and what it is used for.

The next section will help you determine whether you are eligible to register and, if so, walk you through the steps to do so.

Once registered, you will find guidance on how to submit your first job and make the most of the interface.

## Quick Access

LISSY is designed to replicate the experience of working with data on your own machine. Through the interface, you can:

- **Load harmonized datasets** for more than 50 countries worldwide. [R](/r-users/method1.html#import_data_r) | [Stata](/stata-users/method1.html#import_data_stata)

- **Analyse and process data** just as you would locally, with additional access to CPI and PPP deflators provided by LIS. [R](/r-users/dh_cont_vars.html#ppp_in_r) | [Stata](/stata-users/dh_ppp.html#ppp_in_stata)

- **Visualize your results** directly within LISSY. [R](/r-users/tidy_plots.html#plots_in_R) | [Stata](/stata-users/tidy_plots.html#plots_in_stata)

- **Archive your work** and retrieve the outputs of all your past job submissions.

---

For more information on the data available, see:

- **Countries and years** covered by the [LIS Database](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/our-data/lis-database/) and the [LWS Database](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/our-data/lws-database/), also available in [dataset format](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/wp-content/uploads/files/resources_datasets.txt).
- **Variables** — [LIS variables](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/wp-content/uploads/files/data-lis-variables.pdf) and [LWS variables](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/wp-content/uploads/files/data-lws-variables.pdf).
- **Metadata tools** — [METIS](https://www.lisdatacenter.org/frontend#/home) and [Compare.it](https://comparability.lisdatacenter.org/shiny/comparability/).
